message:
feat(fcp-raptorq): add SymbolEnvelope for encrypted symbol transmission

Introduce SymbolEnvelope as the universal transmission unit for encrypted
erasure-coded symbols per FCP Specification Section 4.1.

## New: SymbolEnvelope (envelope.rs)

The SymbolEnvelope is the wire format for transmitting encrypted symbols:

```rust
pub struct SymbolEnvelope {
    pub object_id: ObjectId,      // Content address of complete object
    pub esi: u32,                 // Encoding Symbol ID
    pub k: u16,                   // Source symbols needed (K parameter)
    pub data: Vec<u8>,            // Encrypted symbol payload
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,          // Zone for key derivation
    pub zone_key_id: [u8; 8],     // Zone key rotation identifier
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,        // Replay protection epoch
    pub source_id: NodeId,        // Producing node (NORMATIVE)
    pub sender_instance_id: u64,  // Sender instance identifier
    pub frame_seq: u64,           // Monotonic frame sequence
    pub auth_tag: [u8; 16],       // AEAD authentication tag
}
```

### Nonce Derivation (NORMATIVE)

Two nonce derivation functions for AEAD encryption:

- derive_nonce12(): ChaCha20-Poly1305 (12-byte nonce)
  `nonce = frame_seq_le || esi_le`

- derive_nonce24(): XChaCha20-Poly1305 (24-byte nonce)
  `nonce = sender_instance_id_le || frame_seq_le || esi_le || 0u32`

### Monotonicity Scope

Frame sequences are monotonic within the scope:
`(zone_id, zone_key_id, source_id, sender_instance_id)`

This enables replay detection without global coordination.

## Other Changes

Cargo.toml:
- Add fcp-tailscale dependency for NodeId type

chunk.rs:
- Formatting improvements for readability

config.rs, decode.rs, encode.rs:
- Minor formatting and clippy fixes

golden.rs:
- Improved test formatting

lib.rs:
- Export SymbolEnvelope from crate root

The SymbolEnvelope completes the erasure coding pipeline by providing
a standardized encrypted transport format with built-in replay protection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
